title: cantuffa subvestita
intro: Cantuffa subvestita, also known as the 'Yellow-flowering Cantuffa', is a plant species that belongs to the Bignoniaceae family. It is a climbing woody vine native to tropical regions, recognized for its medicinal properties due to the presence of bioactive compounds such as alkaloids and saponins. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin diseases, with its health benefits attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Cantuffa subvestita can be prepared into herbal remedies such as infusions, decoctions, and ointments for topical application, although its effectiveness and safety should be further studied and validated through scientific research.
